intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

body.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

draft.title: recipe app for people who hate cooking
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
recipe app for people who hate cooking

Building your first draft…

data.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

facade.hypothesis: Users perceive a more patient, less anxious interface when recipe steps load gradually rather than appearing instantly
facade.label: Ingredients fade in
facade.acceptImplies: The app should use staggered entrance animations for ingredients and instructions to create a sense of calm unfolding rather than overwhelming information dump
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer immediate, complete visibility of all recipe content at once—animation would feel slow and patronizing to people already reluctant about cooking

evidence.hypothesis: Users perceive a more patient, less anxious interface when recipe steps load gradually rather than appearing instantly
evidence.implication: The app should use staggered entrance animations for ingredients and instructions to create a sense of calm unfolding rather than overwhelming information dump

facade.hypothesis: People who hate cooking prefer ingredient prep reduced to a simple yes/no verification step rather than reading ingredient paragraphs or hunting through a list.
facade.axisTargeted: Dense ingredient lists vs. scannable checklist format
facade.label: Ingredient checklist
facade.acceptImplies: User values clarity and speed in ingredient verification; recipe app should use cards/checkboxes over paragraphs; minimize cognitive load before cooking starts
facade.rejectImplies: User is comfortable with traditional ingredient paragraphs or wants ingredient details (sourcing, substitutions, quantities) upfront; lists aren't the friction point

facade.hypothesis: A conversational, upbeat bot persona that guides users through recipes with encouragement and jokes—positioning the app as a helpful friend rather than a stern chef.
facade.label: Cheerful robot voice
facade.acceptImplies: Users want a companion tone that makes cooking feel less intimidating; embrace personality and humor in UI copy and voice features.
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer a clean, neutral, get-out-of-my-way interface; focus on clarity and efficiency over personality.

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer interfaces that feel approachable and low-pressure, signaled through warm color temperatures and soft shadows rather than harsh contrast
facade.axisTargeted: Warmth vs. Coldness (color temperature and emotional tone)
facade.label: Warm, soft lighting
facade.acceptImplies: The app should use warm neutrals, soft glows, and gentle gradients to make cooking feel less intimidating and more inviting
facade.rejectImplies: Sharp, cool-toned, clinical interfaces may feel more stressful or sterile for users already anxious about cooking

facade.hypothesis: Users want maximum convenience and speed—they may respond better to interfaces that minimize steps and decisions rather than celebrate the cooking process itself
facade.label: One-tap dinner
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into instant gratification UI patterns: large buttons, immediate results, progress bars, confirmation of quick wins
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer a slower reveal, more browsing, discovery-driven flow, or they want to feel in control of timing rather than rushed

axes.label: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
Whether user wants calm, meditative cooking experience vs. quick-win, dopamine-hit satisfaction from easy wins
Whether user wants minimal information (just do this) vs. detailed explanations (why this works, what you're learning)
axes.poleA: Just tell me or explain why

axes.label: Whether user prefers instant gratification with no waiting vs. enjoying a slower reveal and anticipation of results

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, meditative cooking experience vs. quick-win, dopamine-hit satisfaction from easy wins
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants minimal information (just do this) vs. detailed explanations (why this works, what you're learning)
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers instant gratification with no waiting vs. enjoying a slower reveal and anticipation of results
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking want maximum friction removal—a single interaction to start a recipe, not multiple clicks through menus or navigation.
facade.label: One-tap cooking
facade.acceptImplies: Design around immediate-action primitives: big buttons, voice commands, or swipe-to-start. Minimize decision trees before cooking begins.
facade.rejectImplies: Navigation hierarchy and step-by-step flows are acceptable friction. Users tolerate menus and browsing if the UI is clear and organized.

facade.hypothesis: Users feel more in control and less overwhelmed when recipe instructions are revealed one at a time through gesture rather than presented as a scrollable wall of text
facade.axisTargeted: Gesture-driven progression vs static list viewing
facade.label: Swipe through steps
facade.acceptImplies: The app should use swipe or tap-to-advance interactions for recipe steps, treating instructions like a guided walk-through rather than a reference document
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer seeing the full recipe at once in traditional list format, even if it feels dense, because it lets them skim ahead and plan mentally

draft.title: QuickBite – Recipe App for People Who Hate Cooking
draft.summary: A recipe app that strips away complexity and delivers only the easiest, fastest meals with minimal ingredients and steps. Perfect for busy people who want to eat well without the cooking hassle.
draft.html:
QuickBite

Meals so easy, you'll actually cook them

Garlic Butter Pasta

12 min 4 ingredients Easy

Boil pasta, toss with butter, garlic, and salt. Done.

Weeknight

Avocado Toast

5 min 3 ingredients No cook

Toast bread, mash avocado, spread, add salt and pepper.

Breakfast

Sheet Pan Chicken

25 min 5 ingredients Easy

Throw chicken and veggies on a pan, roast at 400°F.

Dinner
